It is wrong to be happy about the misfortunes of others, especially the people of God; it is a sin for which he will certainly hold you accountable. God will show that he is the God of Israel, even though he allows them to be captives in Babylon for a time. It is better to know him and be poor than to be rich and unaware of him.

Although one event may seem the same to the righteous and the wicked, it is actually very different. Those who boast of any defense or protection other than God’s power, care, and promise will eventually be ashamed of their boasting. If you do not leave vengeance to God, expect that he will take vengeance on you. You should observe the fairness of the Lord's judgments, when he not only avenges injuries upon those who committed them but also against those whom they were done to. Those who hold onto old hatred and wait for an opportunity to show it are storing up wrath for the day of God’s wrath.
